headline: India's AI ambitions hinge on workforce re-skilling, IBM India head says
summary: India will need a coordinated push across government, companies and academia on skilling and ‌policy if it wants to become an AI powerhouse, an IBM ‌executive said, as the technology threatens the country's position as a global services hub.  IBM India head ​Sandip Patel told Reuters on Monday that the South Asian nation's large young workforce could give it an advantage in the global race to adopt and capitalize on the technology, which companies say can improve productivity.  More than half of India's population of around 1.4 billion is under 30, giving the world's most populous nation a vast young workforce.

content.title: India's AI ambitions hinge on workforce re-skilling, IBM India head says

More than half of India's population of around 1.4 billion is under 30, giving the world's most populous nation a vast young workforce.

thumbnail.caption: Sandip Patel, Managing Director, IBM India, gestures during a Reuters summit in Bengaluru

headline: Rigetti (RGTI) Climbs 48% on $100M Govt Backing
summary: Rigetti Computing Inc. (NASDAQ:RGTI) is one of the 10 Stocks With Stunning Double-Digit Gains. Rigetti Computing soared by 48 percent week-on-week, after earning a $100-million financial backing from the government to support US leadership in the quantum space and develop computers capable of solving complex problems.
